Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Is Star Wars really Mayavada in disguise?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2007-08-12 · 4 answers
Check it out:

http://www.gopala.org/node/77

:)
user [2] · 2007-08-13
I would say that is impersonalism, not mayavada. An it is far better than atheism.
user [19] · 2007-08-13
The Matrix is also about impersonalism.

How can they imagine that God is a blue boy who plays the flute.
It is easier to imagine that God is just the force or an energy.
user [78] · 2007-08-13
I like the Matrix. So once my mom gave me an example of the Matrix. She said: those green little numbers. Imagine that those are little Krishnas. So that means that without all those little Krishnas there is nothing.
user [19] · 2007-08-14
Yes it is kind of Krsirodakasayi Visnu. All pervading little Krsnas like all pervading milk.
